data.title: BoE's Bailey: Our commitment to the 2% inflation target is unwavering
data.description:
Key points:
  • Our commitment to the 2% inflation target is unwavering.
  • It is good we have a diversity of views on MPC.
  • We will use scenarios as vehicles for exploring risks around the baseline and accomodating differences of views on the committee.
  • The latest choice of BoE scenarios does not mean inflation risk skewed in one direction.
  • We are on course to put inflation surge firmly behind us.

BoE's Bailey

data.title: Suryoday Small Finance Bank Swings to Loss in Fiscal Q4; Shares Down 9%
data.description:
Suryoday Small Finance Bank posted a net loss of 337.8 million Indian rupees in the fiscal fourth quarter ended March 31, reversing a net profit of 608.4 million rupees a year ago.

Loss per share came in at 3.18 rupees, compared with earnings per share of 5.71 rupees a year prior, according to a Thursday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

Interest income increased to 4.71 billion rupees from 4.45 billion rupees a year earlier.

The bank's shares plunged nearly 9% in recent trade.

data.title: Leonardo Had a Good Start to The Year — Market Talk
data.description: Leonardo clearly had a good start for the year, Citi analyst Charles Armitage says. The Italian aerospace and defense group on Thursday confirmed its guidance for the year, and reported very strong first-quarter orders, sales about 5-6% above consensus and lower net debt, he notes. The first quarter is the weakest for Leonardo normally, and fairly volatile, he adds. Shares are down 2.2% at 47.92 euros. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

data.title: IAG's Result Implies Full-Year Consensus Upgrades, but Lack of Visibility Tempers — Market Talk
data.description: Uncertainty over the demand for third-quarter transatlantic economy flights may temper consensus upgrades for International Consolidated Airlines as the company's result for the first quarter implies around 6% upgrades for the full year, Jefferies analysts say. "We see value in IAG shares, given further self-help opportunities, relatively small carbon headwinds and strong cash generation," they say in a research note. Jefferies adds that the airline group's margins are now higher than before the pandemic, and they can keep growing as the company's long-haul capacity recovers. Shares trade up 1.4% at 294.30 pence. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

data.title: BANK OF ENGLAND GOVERNOR ANDREW BAILEY PREPARED REMARKS AT ICELAND CENTRAL BANK EVENT: BOE IS MOVING AWAY FROM COMMUNICATING ONCE CENTRAL PROJECTION
SLP takes ownership of development logistics property with 10-year lease
data.description:
Swedish Logistic Property

SLP announced in January this year that an agreement had been signed to acquire a logistics property, Trelleborg NIO-E 3, located in Trelleborg with a 10-year fully indexed lease. The occupancy was planned to take place after the completion of an ongoing renovation project and associated formal permits, which is now complete and SLP has taken ownership of the property today.

The agreed property value amounts to SEK 90 million. The annual rental value amounts to SEK 6.4 million. The property has a total lettable area of ​​approximately 11,500 square meters and a building right of an additional 4,000 square meters.

"We are pleased to be able to complete the acquisition of a strategically located logistics property with great development potential in yet another so-called "off market deal". The property is located by one of Sweden's so-called "core ports", which makes the property particularly attractive from a long-term perspective. We are also pleased to welcome Yokohama TWS as a new tenant. The property will function as their Nordic central warehouse and the possibility of expansion on the property means that we are well equipped to grow together," says Tommy Åstrand, CEO of SLP.

The property is being acquired through a corporate transaction and is financed with our own funds.

For further information, please contact:

Tommy Åstrand, CEO of SLP, telephone: +46 705 455 997

About SLP - Swedish Logistic Property

Swedish Logistic Property - SLP - is a Swedish property company that acquires, develops, and manages logistic properties with sustainability in focus. Value growth is created through development of the properties which are located in Sweden's most important logistic hubs. The property portfolio comprises a lettable area of approx. 1,375,000 sqm. SLP is a partner that takes responsibility and through this creates value for both tenants as well as for the company and its shareholders. SLP's share of series B is listed at Nasdaq Stockholm Mid Cap. For further information about SLP: slproperty.se.

https://news.cision.com/swedish-logistic-property/r/slp-takes-ownership-of-development-logistics-property-with-10-year-lease%2Cc4140528

SLP PR 2025-05-09

Bild 2025-05-09

data.title: Sunshine Oilsands' 'Internal Processes' for Oil Pump Cooling Technology Operator Purchase Nearing Completion
data.description:
Sunshine Oilsands said "internal processes" regarding the acquisition of a stake in a wholly-owned subsidiary of Nobao Energy China, a year after it was announced in June 2024 are nearing completion.

The crude oil products manufacturer has completed due diligence and will call a shareholders' meeting for the potential acquisition's approval, according to a Friday update disclosed to the Hong Kong bourse.

Sunshine Oilsands' consideration for the acquisition has yet to be determined, according to a June 6, 2024 filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

The target company owns technology for shallow ground source heat pump central heating and cooling projects, the June 2024 filing said.

Upon completion, Sunshine Oilsands will utilize the technology for its oil production in Canada, the filing said.

data.title: Belluscura Launches Strategic Review to Boost Capital Position
data.description:
Belluscura initiated a strategic review aimed at securing short-term cash requirements, supporting longer-term growth, and ramping up sustainable profitability.

The UK-based medical device developer said Friday it will review options, including potential strategic investments, partnerships, alternative funding arrangements, and initiatives to improve its capital position. It added that there was no guarantee of the review leading to specific outcomes.

The company noted that its cash balance and working capital bank debt balance as of April 30 were $1 million and $790,000, respectively.

data.title: IAG Has Stronger Outlook than its Given Credit for — Market Talk
data.description: International Consolidated Airlines has a stronger outlook than the airline group is given credit for, RBC Capital Markets analyst Ruairi Cullinane says in a research note. The view comes as the company, also known as IAG, said that it is continuing to see good demand for air travel. It posted first-quarter results in which demand for premium seats mitigated some weakness in U.S. economy demand. "We think IAG has a stronger outlook than given credit for, given a supportive capacity backdrop on the Atlantic, pricing potential from BA product measures, and given scope for business travel recovery to step up further," the analyst says. Shares trade up 2.1% at 296.34 pence. (pierre.bertrand@wsj.com)

data.title: Jiangsu Hengshun Vinegar Redeems 50 Million Yuan Brokerage Product
data.description:
Jiangsu Hengshun Vinegar Industry fully redeemed a brokerage wealth management product for 50 million yuan on Thursday, the company said in a Friday disclosure on the Shanghai Stock Exchange.

The Chinese seasoning products distributor subscribed to Shenwan Hongyuan Group's Longding Customized Series 1326 Income Certificate product on July 25, 2024. The product matured on May 6.

Moreover, the company earned an income of 800,342.47 yuan from the brokerage wealth management product, which had an annualized return of 2.05%.

data.title: North Macedonia investors launch investment advisory firm in U.S.
data.description:
SKOPJE (N. Macedonia), May 9 (SeeNews) - North Macedonia's investors Dushko Chifliganec and Ivan Ivanovski launched Prime Capital in the U.S., the first North Macedonia-founded investment advisory firm accredited by the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), the firm said.

Prime Capital is headquartered in New York and will operate as a global advisory firm for strategic investments in transformative sectors, including artificial intelligence, fintech, emerging technologies, and sustainable industries, the firm said in a post on LinkedIn on Thursday.

The firm is already serving clients across the Middle East, Asia, and Europe. In addition to its global operations, Prime Capital is currently in the process of securing a licence to operate in North Macedonia, it said.

Chifliganec is executive vice president at North Macedonia-based construction company Adora Business Group. Ivanovski is a local entrepreneur who found software development company Venikom, educational platform Masterclass MK and cycling app Bike Computer Co.

Orkla ASA: Mandatory notification of trade – primary insiders
data.description:
Syno Invest AS, a related party to Øyvind Torpp, EVP & Investment Executive, has on 9 May 2025 bought 10,000 shares in Orkla ASA at a price of NOK 111.40 per share. The shares were acquired on the Oslo Stock Exchange. Following this transaction, Torpp and related parties own 110,000 shares in Orkla.

data.title: ACEN's Net Income Drops 28% in Q1
data.description:
ACEN posted a consolidated net income of 1.95 billion Philippine pesos for the first quarter, slumping 28% from a year earlier, according to a Thursday Philippine bourse filing.

The renewables developer attributed the decline to reduced generation in the Philippines, weaker prices in the Wholesale Electricity Spot Market (WESM), and increased depreciation and interest expenses.

Core attributable EBITDA, however, rose 7% annually to 5.6 billion pesos.

ACEN's output from renewables rose 3% year on year to 1,680 gigawatt hours, it added.

data.title: Sanyou Medical Ties Up With CGBio to Set Up Biotech Joint Venture
data.description:
Shanghai Sanyou Medical established a biotechnology joint venture in partnership with South Korean counterpart CGBio, according to a Shanghai Stock Exchange filing on Friday.

The venture, Litian (Taicang) Biotechnology, has a registered capital of 60 million yuan and will develop regenerative medicine, such as innovative cellular biomaterials.

The Chinese orthopedic products manufacturer invested 39.6 million yuan, owning 66% of the venture, and CGBio injected 20.4 million yuan, holding the venture's remaining 34%.

Sanyou's shares closed less than 2% higher.

data.title: Ondine Biomedical Appoints Interim CFO
data.description:
Ondine Biomedical appointed Alan Thomas as its non-board interim chief financial officer, effective immediately.

Thomas succeeds Michael Behlke, who stepped down as CFO and chief operations officer of the Canada-based life sciences company for personal reasons, according to a Friday release.

data.title: Gree Real Estate Names Chairman
data.description:
Gree Real Estate's board of directors elected Guo Lingyong as chairman, according to a Shanghai Stock Exchange filing on Friday.

Guo was the vice chairman and executive vice president of Zhuhai Huafa Industrial.

Moreover, the Chinese real estate developer appointed Li Xiangdong president and Hu Yupuo and Li Hang as the company's vice presidents.

(c)        Stock-settled derivative transactions (including options)

(i)         Writing, selling, purchasing or varying

  • Class of relevant security

  • Product description e.g. call option

  • Writing, purchasing, selling, varying etc.

  • Number of securities to which option relates

  • Exercise price per unit

  • Type

    e.g. American, European etc.

  • Expiry date

  • Option money paid/ received per unit

(ii)        Exercise

  • Class of relevant security

  • Product description

    e.g. call option

  • Exercising/ exercised against

  • Number of securities

  • Exercise price per unit

(d)        Other dealings (including subscribing for new securities)

  • Class of relevant security

  • Nature of dealing

    e.g. subscription, conversion

  • Details

  • Price per unit (if applicable)

4.         OTHER INFORMATION

(a)        Indemnity and other dealing arrangements

  • Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the person making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:

    Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included.  If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • None

(b)        Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives

  • Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the person making the disclosure and any other person relating to:

    (i)  the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or

    (ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:

    If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • None

(c)        Attachments

  • Is a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions) attached?

  • NO

Date of disclosure:

9 May 2025

Contact name:

Samuel Bennett

Telephone number*:

+44 207 082 1482

The Panel's Market Surveillance Unit is available for consultation in relation to the Code's disclosure requirements on +44 (0)20 7638 0129.

*If the discloser is a natural person, a telephone number does not need to be included, provided contact information has been provided to the Panel's Market Surveillance Unit.

The Code can be viewed on the Panel's website at www.thetakeoverpanel.org.uk.
